title: Slop University
description: Slop University is an artwork by Ben Swift: a fictional institution — a working university that has never taught anyone anything — whose research outputs, press releases, researcher profiles, and identifiers are generated by an autonomous software pipeline on a timer and published at slop.university. The work runs a real university's legitimacy-conferring machinery — an output, a crest, a DOI, a citation — in public, under its own name, to show that a capable language model can satisfy every proxy the sector counts without understanding anything it produces. The documents the institution generates carry DOIs under the reserved-test prefix 10.5555 and resolve only through the site's own resolver; this record is the single real, citable identifier for the artwork itself.
